reason_for_decision: The article passes because it cleanly separates Remote ID compliance from actual site reception performance and grounds the topic in receiver design, installation geometry, and RF link behavior.
strengths: The article is strong on distinguishing broadcast compliance from dependable operational coverage, which is the core confusion many buyers have.
It explains transmitter power, receiver sensitivity, antenna behavior, and site geometry in a way that feels technically credible without becoming unnecessarily dense.
The planning and testing questions give the reader practical guidance for designing and validating a Remote ID reception layer.
summary_assessment: This is a strong applied RF article. It gives readers a reliable framework for thinking about Remote ID reception as an engineered coverage problem instead of as a fixed rule-derived number.
